title: "2026-01-29 AK Flow Cytometry Analysis: Tasso Project"

output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Introduction

The following analysis is a cleaned up example for how I perform a quick overview analysis on flow cytometry data. The main idea is to have statistics of parameters consistent across all cell populations in a given experiment. Tables consisting of statistics are exported from FlowJo for each population and then joined together in R to make one analyses table.Before importing tables generated from FlowJo into R, parameter names have been renamed to use language more compatible with the R language. For my analysis I try to use the following language "CD69_Percent_Positive" or "CD69_MFI" or "CD69_MFI_of_CD69_Positive".

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

```

# Todo log


# Load Libraries

```{r load_libraries}

# Libraries to manipulate data
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)

# Libraries associated with plotting 
library(ggplot2)
library(pheatmap)
library(viridis)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggsignif)
library(ggsci)
library(scales)

# Stats library
library(ggstatsplot)

# Visit website for any library you are unfamiliar with to learn its usage

# Set Seed

set.seed(123)

```

# Set Directories

In every R analysis, I use the following directory set up for organization

Level 1:

Folder 1 "Main Directory with the title of experiment"

Level 2:

Folder 1 "code" contains this Rmd file and an R project file to save image of work space

Folder 2 "background" contains any relevant files needed to interpret experiment, papers etc.

Folder 3 "data_input" contains any data we plan to load into R

Folder 4 "data_output" will contain any tabular data we create from R

Folder 5 "plots" will contain any plots we save from R

If this is done, then the following code block gets the name of the working directory this file has been created in and then uses that to load the directories needed for the following analysis.

```{r set_directories}

# Set directories
dirname(getwd()) 
dir.base <- file.path(dirname(getwd()))
dir.data_input <- file.path(dir.base, "data_input")
dir.data_output <- file.path(dir.base, "data_output")
dir.plots <- file.path(dir.base, "plots")

```

# Make Custom Functions

I use a couple custom functions throughout different analysis. I load them upfront in case I need them.

```{r custom_functions}

`%notin%` <- Negate(`%in%`)
sum_rm_na <- function(x){sum(x, na.rm = TRUE)}

calculate_ci <- function(x, confidence_level = 0.95) {

  n <- length(x)

  mean_x <- mean(x)

  sd_x <- sd(x)

  critical_value <- qt(confidence_level + (1 - confidence_level) / 2, df = n - 1)

  margin_of_error <- critical_value * (sd_x / sqrt(n))

  lower_bound <- mean_x - margin_of_error

  upper_bound <- mean_x + margin_of_error

  return(paste(sprintf("%.1f", lower_bound), sprintf("%.1f", upper_bound), sep = " - "))

}

calculate_iqr <- function(x) {

  lower_bound <- quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)

  upper_bound <- quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  iqr_number <- upper_bound - lower_bound

  return(paste(sprintf("%.1f", iqr_number)))

}


```

# Load Data

The following code block will load data I have prepared for this analysis that is located in a folder named "clean" inside the "data_input" folder. When reading in the tables the following code will add an additional column with the population name for the cell type that the statistics where generated from.

```{r load_data}

# Load data

list.files(file.path(dir.data_input))

dir.data_input_01 <- file.path(dir.data_input, "2022-08-22 AK Tasso Pre Pilot Fresh T cell Panel")
dir.data_input_02 <- file.path(dir.data_input, "2022-08-29 AK Tasso Pre Pilot Frozen T cell Panel")
dir.data_input_03 <- file.path(dir.data_input, "2022-09-02 AK Tasso Pilot T cell Panel")
dir.data_input_04 <- file.path(dir.data_input, "2022-10-28 AK Tasso Batch 1 T cell Panel")
dir.data_input_05 <- file.path(dir.data_input, "2024-11-14 AK Tasso Batch 2 T cell Panel")
dir.data_input_06 <- file.path(dir.data_input, "2024-11-22 AK Meta Time")

## Load meta data related to the time the second tasso was in transit

list.files(file.path(dir.data_input,"2024-11-22 AK Meta Time"))

data_meta_time <- read_csv(file.path(dir.data_input_06, "2025-04-03 AK Tasso Meta Data Shipping.csv"))

data_meta_time <- data_meta_time %>% #Tasso ID 16 was skipped in the collection process and doesn't exist
  filter(Donor != "Tasso ID 16")

## Load meta data, population frequency data, and phenotyping frequency data

### 01 
#### All data_input repositories are organised the same. Figure out 1 and repeat for the rest.

list.files(dir.data_input_01)

data_meta_01 <- read_csv(file.path(dir.data_input_01, "00.csv"), 
                        col_select = c(1:4))

meta_colnames <- data.frame("Old" = colnames(data_meta_01))

meta_colnames$New <- c("File_Name",
                       "Date",
                       "Donor",
                       "Group")

data_meta_01 <- read_csv(file.path(dir.data_input_01, "00.csv"), 
                        skip = 1,
                        col_select = c(1:4),
                        col_names = meta_colnames$New)

data_frequency_01 <- read_csv(file.path(dir.data_input_01,"01.csv"),
                              col_select = c(1:61))

frequency_colnames <- data.frame("Old" = colnames(data_frequency_01))

frequency_colnames$New <- c("File_Name",
                            "Live_Percent_of_Single_Cells",
                            "CD45_Percent_Positive_of_Live",
                            
                            "B_cells_Total_Percent_of_CD45_Positive",
                            "T_cells_Total_Percent_of_CD45_Positive",
                        
                            "TCRgd_T_cells_Total_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "TCRgd_T_cells_Naive_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "TCRgd_T_cells_TEMRA_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "TCRgd_T_cells_CM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "TCRgd_T_cells_EM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "TCRgd_T_cells_Total_Memory_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                        
                            "MAIT_cells_Total_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "MAIT_cells_Naive_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "MAIT_T_cells_TEMRA_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "MAIT_T_cells_CM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "MAIT_T_cells_EM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "MAIT_T_cells_Total_Memory_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                        
                            "Treg_cells_Total_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Treg_cells_Naive_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Treg_cells_TEMRA_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Treg_cells_CM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Treg_cells_EM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Treg_cells_Total_Memory_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                        
                            "Conventional_CD4_T_cells_Total_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Conventional_CD4_T_cells_Naive_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Conventional_CD4_T_cells_TEMRA_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Conventional_CD4_T_cells_CM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Conventional_CD4_T_cells_EM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Conventional_CD4_T_cells_Total_Memory_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                        
                            "Conventional_CD8_T_cells_Total_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Conventional_CD8_T_cells_Naive_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Conventional_CD8_T_cells_TEMRA_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Conventional_CD8_T_cells_CM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Conventional_CD8_T_cells_EM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Conventional_CD8_T_cells_Total_Memory_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                        
                            "CD4_CD8_Double_Negative_T_cells_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                        
                            "Naive_Percent_of_TCRgd_T_cells_Total",
                            "TEMRA_Percent_of_TCRgd_T_cells_Total",
                            "CM_Percent_of_TCRgd_T_cells_Total",
                            "EM_Percent_of_TCRgd_T_cells_Total",
                            "Total_Memory_Percent_of_TCRgd_T_cells_Total",
                        
                            "Naive_Percent_of_MAIT_cells_Total",
                            "TEMRA_Percent_of_MAIT_cells_Total",
                            "CM_Percent_of_MAIT_cells_Total",
                            "EM_Percent_of_MAIT_cells_Total",
                            "Total_Memory_Percent_of_MAIT_cells_Total",
                        
                            "Naive_Percent_of_Treg_cells_Total",
                            "TEMRA_Percent_of_Treg_cells_Total",
                            "CM_Percent_of_Treg_cells_Total",
                            "EM_Percent_of_Treg_cells_Total",
                            "Total_Memory_Percent_of_Treg_cells_Total",
                      
                            "Naive_Percent_of_Conventional_CD4_T_cells_Total",
                            "TEMRA_Percent_of_Conventional_CD4_T_cells_Total",
                            "CM_Percent_of_Conventional_CD4_T_cells_Total",
                            "EM_Percent_of_Conventional_CD4_T_cells_Total",
                            "Total_Memory_Percent_of_Conventional_CD4_T_cells_Total",
                      
                            "Naive_Percent_of_Conventional_CD8_T_cells_Total",
                            "TEMRA_Percent_of_Conventional_CD8_T_cells_Total",
                            "CM_Percent_of_Conventional_CD8_T_cells_Total",
                            "EM_Percent_of_Conventional_CD8_T_cells_Total",
                            "Total_Memory_Percent_of_Conventional_CD8_T_cells_Total")
                                                                                                                                        
data_frequency_01 <- read_csv(file.path(dir.data_input_01,"01.csv"),
                              skip =1,
                              col_select = c(1:61),
                              col_names = frequency_colnames$New)

data_counts_01 <- read_csv(file.path(dir.data_input_01,"02.csv"),
                              col_select = c(1:37))

count_colnames <- data.frame("Old" = colnames(data_counts_01))

count_colnames$New <- c("File_Name",
                        "Ungated_Count",
                        "Live_Count",
                        "CD45_Positive_Count",
                    
                        "B_cells_Total_Count",
                        "T_cells_Total_Count",
                    
                        "TCRgd_T_cells_Total_Count",
                        "TCRgd_T_cells_Naive_Count",
                        "TCRgd_T_cells_TEMRA_Count",
                        "TCRgd_T_cells_CM_Count",
                        "TCRgd_T_cells_EM_Count",
                        "TCRgd_T_cells_Total_Memory_Count",
                    
                        "MAIT_cells_Total_Count",

                        "Conventional_CD4_T_cells_Naive_Count",
                        "Conventional_CD4_T_cells_TEMRA_Count",
                        "Conventional_CD4_T_cells_CM_Count",
                        "Conventional_CD4_T_cells_EM_Count",
                        "Conventional_CD4_T_cells_Total_Memory_Count",
                    
                        "Conventional_CD8_T_cells_Total_Count",
                        "Conventional_CD8_T_cells_Naive_Count",
                        "Conventional_CD8_T_cells_TEMRA_Count",
                        "Conventional_CD8_T_cells_CM_Count",
                        "Conventional_CD8_T_cells_EM_Count",
                        "Conventional_CD8_T_cells_Total_Memory_Count",
                    
                        "CD4_CD8_Double_Negative_T_cells_Count"
                        )

data_counts_01 <- read_csv(file.path(dir.data_input_01,"02.csv"),
                           skip = 1,
                           col_select = c(1:37),
                           col_names = count_colnames$New)

data_phen_pop01_01 <- read_csv(file.path(dir.data_input_01,"03.csv"),
                               col_select = c(1:19)) %>%
  mutate(Population = "TCRgd T cells Total")

phen_colnames <- data.frame("Old" = colnames(data_phen_pop01_01))
```

```{r weather}

# "2022-09-01 00:53:00 UTC" > "2022-08-30 08:35:00 UTC"
# # TRUE
# 
# "2022-09-01 00:53:00 UTC" > as.POSIXct("2022-09-26 11:39:00 UTC")
# # FALSE

# New is greater than old, treating time as character

data_weather <- read_csv(file.path(dir.data_input,"2024-11-22 AK Meta Time", "3914773.csv"))

data_weather_sub <- data_weather %>%
  select(STATION, DATE, REPORT_TYPE...3, HourlyDryBulbTemperature) %>%
  filter(STATION != "SOD")

data_weather_summary <- data.frame(Donor = character(), Group = character(), Min_Transit_Temp = double(), Max_Transit_Temp = double(), Mean_Transit_Temp = double())

donor_sub <- unique(data_meta_time$Donor)[unique(data_meta_time$Donor) %notin% c("Tasso ID 01", "Tasso ID 02", "Tasso ID 03", "Tasso ID 04", "Tasso ID 16")]

for (i in donor_sub){
  print(i)
  # if(i %in% c("Tasso ID 01", "Tasso ID 02", "Tasso ID 03", "Tasso ID 04", "Tasso ID 16")) next
  
  draw <- data_meta_time %>% filter(Group == "Tasso WB Shipped" & Donor == i) %>% select(`Draw Date Time`) %>% .[[1]]
  rec <- data_meta_time %>% filter(Group == "Tasso WB Shipped" & Donor == i) %>% select(`Received Date Time`) %>% .[[1]]
  
  data_weather_sub_1 <- data_weather_sub %>%
    filter(DATE > draw & DATE < rec)
  
  
  data_weather_summary_temp <- data.frame(Donor = i, 
                                          Group = "Tasso WB Shipped", 
                                          Min_Transit_Temp = min(data_weather_sub_1$HourlyDryBulbTemperature, na.rm = TRUE), 
                                          Max_Transit_Temp = max(data_weather_sub_1$HourlyDryBulbTemperature, na.rm = TRUE), 
                                          Mean_Transit_Temp = mean(data_weather_sub_1$HourlyDryBulbTemperature, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  data_weather_summary <- rbind(data_weather_summary, data_weather_summary_temp)
  
}

max(data_weather_summary$Max_Transit_Temp)
min(data_weather_summary$Min_Transit_Temp)

data_meta_time <- data_meta_time %>%
  full_join(data_weather_summary)

write_csv(x = data_meta_time,
          file = file.path(dir.data_output, "data_meta_time_weather.csv"))

```


# Organize and Clean Data

The following code will combine all the phenotyping data for each population into one table and then add the meta data.

New variables will be created from the meta data to assist with sub-setting later, most often this is creating one variable from two i.e."Time Point" + "Stim" = "Time Point Stim".

Meta data variable will be factorized. This gives an order for the variables to appear when plotting. i.e. The order of variables for "Stim" to appear on the x-axis should be "50 ng", "5 ng", "0.5 ng", "0.1 ng", and "no stim".

Last, I will be converting NA values to 0. In this experiment the only NA values occur in variable that fall under the statistics like "PARAMETER_MFI_of_PARAMETER_Positive". This is because some in some instances there are no positive events in said gate and so FlowJo could not output a MFI on no cells. For plotting purposes I have chosen to treat these as "0", but users need to define how to treat NAs for themselves. (Not doing for the current analysis)

```{r organize_and_clean_data}

# Clean up data and add new variables

## Make one table
table(data_phen_pop01_01$File_Name == data_phen_pop02_01$File_Name)

data_phen_all <- data_phen_pop01_01 %>%
  bind_rows(data_phen_pop02_01,
            data_phen_pop03_01,
            data_phen_pop04_01,
            data_phen_pop05_01,
            data_phen_pop06_01,
            data_phen_pop07_01,
            data_phen_pop08_01,
            data_phen_pop09_01,
            data_phen_pop10_01,
            data_phen_pop11_01,
            data_phen_pop12_01,
            data_phen_pop13_01,
            data_phen_pop14_01,
            
            data_phen_pop01_02,
            data_phen_pop02_02,
            data_phen_pop03_02,
            data_phen_pop04_02,
            data_phen_pop05_02,
            data_phen_pop06_02,
            data_phen_pop07_02,
            data_phen_pop08_02,
            data_phen_pop09_02,
            data_phen_pop10_02,
            data_phen_pop11_02,
            data_phen_pop12_02,
            data_phen_pop13_02,
            data_phen_pop14_02,
            
            data_phen_pop01_03,
            data_phen_pop02_03,
            data_phen_pop03_03,
            data_phen_pop04_03,
            data_phen_pop05_03,
            data_phen_pop06_03,
            data_phen_pop07_03,
            data_phen_pop08_03,
            data_phen_pop09_03,
            data_phen_pop10_03,
            data_phen_pop11_03,
            data_phen_pop12_03,
            data_phen_pop13_03,
            data_phen_pop14_03,
            
            data_phen_pop01_04,
            data_phen_pop02_04,
            data_phen_pop03_04,
            data_phen_pop04_04,
            data_phen_pop05_04,
            data_phen_pop06_04,
            data_phen_pop07_04,
            data_phen_pop08_04,
            data_phen_pop09_04,
            data_phen_pop10_04,
            data_phen_pop11_04,
            data_phen_pop12_04,
            data_phen_pop13_04,
            data_phen_pop14_04,
            
            data_phen_pop01_05,
            data_phen_pop02_05,
            data_phen_pop03_05,
            data_phen_pop04_05,
            data_phen_pop05_05,
            data_phen_pop06_05,
            data_phen_pop07_05,
            data_phen_pop08_05,
            data_phen_pop09_05,
            data_phen_pop10_05,
            data_phen_pop11_05,
            data_phen_pop12_05,
            data_phen_pop13_05,
            data_phen_pop14_05,
            )

data_frequency_all <- data_frequency_01 %>%
  bind_rows(data_frequency_02,
            data_frequency_03,
            data_frequency_04,
            data_frequency_05)

data_frequency_all_b_cells <- data_frequency_04_b_cells %>% #Added for review comments 2025-12-30 AK
  bind_rows(data_frequency_05_b_cells) #Added for review comments 2025-12-30 AK

data_counts_all <- data_counts_01 %>%
  bind_rows(data_counts_02,
            data_counts_03,
            data_counts_04,
            data_counts_05)

data_meta_all <- data_meta_01 %>%
  bind_rows(data_meta_02,
            data_meta_03,
            data_meta_04,
            data_meta_05)

table(data_meta_all$Donor, data_meta_all$Group)

data_phen_all <- data_phen_all %>%
  full_join(data_meta_all)

data_phen_all <- data_phen_all %>%
  full_join(data_meta_time)

data_frequency_all <- data_frequency_all %>%
  full_join(data_meta_all)

data_frequency_all <- data_frequency_all %>%
  full_join(data_meta_time)

data_frequency_all <- data_frequency_all %>% #Added for review comments 2025-12-30 AK
  full_join(data_frequency_all_b_cells) #Added for review comments 2025-12-30 AK


data.frame(colnames(data_counts_all))
data.frame(unique(data_phen_all$Population))

index_count <- c(1, 5, 7, 13, 19, 26:30, 32:36)
data_counts_phen_all <- data_counts_all[,index_count]

data_counts_phen_all_colnames <- data.frame("Old" = colnames(data_counts_phen_all))
data.frame(colnames(data_counts_phen_all))
data_counts_phen_all_colnames$New <- c("File_Name",
                                       "B cells Total",
                                       "TCRgd T cells Total",
                                       "MAIT cells Total",
                                       "Tregs Total",
                                       
                                       "Conventional CD4 Naive",
                                       "Conventional CD4 CM",
                                       "Conventional CD4 EM",
                                       "Conventional CD4 TEMRA",
                                       "Conventional CD4 Total Memory",
                                       
                                       "Conventional CD8 Naive",
                                       "Conventional CD8 CM",
                                       "Conventional CD8 EM",
                                       "Conventional CD8 TEMRA",
                                       "Conventional CD8 Total Memory")

unique(data_phen_all$Population) %in% data_counts_phen_all_colnames$New

colnames(data_counts_phen_all) <- data_counts_phen_all_colnames$New

data_counts_phen_all <- data_counts_phen_all %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = data_counts_phen_all_colnames$New[2:15],
               names_to = "Population",
               values_to = "Count")

data_phen_all <- data_phen_all %>%
  full_join(data_counts_phen_all)

## Change variable name

### Parameters to filter sets by 

data_phen_all <- data_phen_all %>%
  mutate(Donor_Population_Date = paste(Donor, Population, Date))

data_phen_all <- data_phen_all %>%
  mutate(Filter = case_when(
    Count < 20 ~ TRUE,
    .default = FALSE
  ))

filtered_tasso_id <- data_frequency_all %>%
  filter(Date == "28-OCT-2022" | Date == "14-NOV-2024") %>%
  filter(Group == "Tasso WB Shipped") %>%
  filter(`Transit Hours` < 48) %>%
  filter(Live_Percent_of_Single_Cells > 70)

filtered_tasso_id <- unique(filtered_tasso_id$Donor)
length(filtered_tasso_id)

table(data_phen_all[data_phen_all$Filter==TRUE,]$Population)
table(data_phen_all[data_phen_all$Filter==TRUE,]$Group,data_phen_all[data_phen_all$Filter==TRUE,]$Population)

## Factorize variables for plotting order

### Phenotyping table

colnames(data_meta_all)

table(data_phen_all$File_Name, data_phen_all$Group)
data.frame(unique(data_phen_all$File_Name))
data_phen_all <- data_phen_all %>%
  mutate(Group = case_when(
    File_Name == "2022-08-19_SAC253053 Day 2.fcs" ~ "Control Shipped",
    File_Name == "2022-08-30_2022-09-01_SAC253053 D2.fcs" ~ "Control Shipped",
    .default = Group
  ))
table(data_phen_all$File_Name, data_phen_all$Group)

unique(data_meta_all$Donor)
data_phen_all$Donor <- factor(data_phen_all$Donor, levels =c("Tasso ID 01",
                                                             "Tasso ID 02",
                                                             "Tasso ID 03",
                                                             "Tasso ID 04",
                                                             "Tasso ID 05",
                                                             "Tasso ID 06",
                                                             "Tasso ID 07",
                                                             "Tasso ID 08",
                                                             "Tasso ID 09",
                                                             "Tasso ID 10",
                                                             "Tasso ID 11",
                                                             "Tasso ID 12",
                                                             "Tasso ID 13",
                                                             "Tasso ID 14",
                                                             "Tasso ID 15",
                                                             "Tasso ID 17",
                                                             "Tasso ID 18",
                                                             "Tasso ID 19",
                                                             "Tasso ID 20",
                                                             "Tasso ID 21",
                                                             "SAC253053"))

unique(data_meta_all$Date)
data_phen_all$Date <- factor(data_phen_all$Date, levels = c("22-AUG-2022",
                                                            "29-AUG-2022",
                                                            "02-SEP-2022",
                                                            "28-OCT-2022",
                                                            "14-NOV-2024"))

unique(data_meta_all$Group)
data_phen_all$Group <- factor(data_phen_all$Group, levels = c("Control",
                                                              "Control Shipped",
                                                              "Vein PBMC Pre",
                                                              "Vein PBMC",
                                                              "Vein WB",
                                                              "Tasso WB",
                                                              "Tasso WB Shipped"))

unique(data_phen_all$Population)
  
data_phen_all$Population <- factor(data_phen_all$Population, levels = c("TCRgd T cells Total",
                                                                        "MAIT cells Total",
                                                                        "Tregs Total",
                                                                        "Conventional CD4 Naive",
                                                                        "Conventional CD4 CM",
                                                                        "Conventional CD4 EM",
                                                                        "Conventional CD4 TEMRA",
                                                                        "Conventional CD4 Total Memory",
                                                                        "Conventional CD8 Naive",
                                                                        "Conventional CD8 CM",
                                                                        "Conventional CD8 EM",
                                                                        "Conventional CD8 TEMRA",
                                                                        "Conventional CD8 Total Memory",
                                                                        "B cells Total"))

data_phen_all <- data_phen_all %>%
  mutate(Experiment = case_when(
    Date == "22-AUG-2022" ~ 1,
    Date == "29-AUG-2022" ~ 1,
    Date == "02-SEP-2022" ~ 2,
    Date == "28-OCT-2022" ~ 3,
    Date == "14-NOV-2024" ~ 3
  ))

### Frequency table

colnames(data_meta_all)

table(data_frequency_all$File_Name, data_frequency_all$Group)
data.frame(unique(data_frequency_all$File_Name))
data_frequency_all <- data_frequency_all %>%
  mutate(Group = case_when(
    File_Name == "2022-08-19_SAC253053 Day 2.fcs" ~ "Control Shipped",
    File_Name == "2022-08-30_2022-09-01_SAC253053 D2.fcs" ~ "Control Shipped",
    .default = Group
  ))
table(data_frequency_all$File_Name, data_frequency_all$Group)

unique(data_meta_all$Donor)
data_frequency_all$Donor <- factor(data_frequency_all$Donor, levels =c("Tasso ID 01",
                                                             "Tasso ID 02",
                                                             "Tasso ID 03",
                                                             "Tasso ID 04",
                                                             "Tasso ID 05",
                                                             "Tasso ID 06",
                                                             "Tasso ID 07",
                                                             "Tasso ID 08",
                                                             "Tasso ID 09",
                                                             "Tasso ID 10",
                                                             "Tasso ID 11",
                                                             "Tasso ID 12",
                                                             "Tasso ID 13",
                                                             "Tasso ID 14",
                                                             "Tasso ID 15",
                                                             "Tasso ID 17",
                                                             "Tasso ID 18",
                                                             "Tasso ID 19",
                                                             "Tasso ID 20",
                                                             "Tasso ID 21",
                                                             "SAC253053"))

unique(data_meta_all$Date)
data_frequency_all$Date <- factor(data_frequency_all$Date, levels = c("22-AUG-2022",
                                                            "29-AUG-2022",
                                                            "02-SEP-2022",
                                                            "28-OCT-2022",
                                                            "14-NOV-2024"))

unique(data_meta_all$Group)
data_frequency_all$Group <- factor(data_frequency_all$Group, levels = c("Control",
                                                                        "Control Shipped",
                                                                        "Vein PBMC Pre",
                                                                        "Vein PBMC",
                                                                        "Vein WB",
                                                                        "Tasso WB",
                                                                        "Tasso WB Shipped"))

data_frequency_all <- data_frequency_all %>%
  mutate(Experiment = case_when(
    Date == "22-AUG-2022" ~ 1,
    Date == "29-AUG-2022" ~ 1,
    Date == "02-SEP-2022" ~ 2,
    Date == "28-OCT-2022" ~ 3,
    Date == "14-NOV-2024" ~ 3
  ))

## Order tables by donor so that paired comparisons match correctly

data_phen_all <- data_phen_all %>%
  arrange(Donor, Group)

data_frequency_all <- data_frequency_all %>%
  arrange(Donor, Group)

##

## Replace NA which occur with MFIs to 0 so we can perform paired analysis of MFI

# data_phen_all[is.na(data_phen_all)] <- 0
# not using AK 2024-11-25

## Donor Colors

paletteer::paletteer_d("ggsci::default_igv")[1:21]

donor_colors <- scale_color_manual(values = c("Tasso ID 01" = "#5050FFFF",
                                              "Tasso ID 02" = "#CE3D32FF",
                                              "Tasso ID 03" = "#749B58FF",
                                              "Tasso ID 04" = "#F0E685FF",
                                              "Tasso ID 05" = "#466983FF",
                                              "Tasso ID 06" = "#BA6338FF",
                                              "Tasso ID 07" = "#5DB1DDFF",
                                              "Tasso ID 08" = "#802268FF",
                                              "Tasso ID 09" = "#6BD76BFF",
                                              "Tasso ID 10" = "#D595A7FF",
                                              "Tasso ID 11" = "#924822FF",
                                              "Tasso ID 12" = "#837B8DFF",
                                              "Tasso ID 13" = "#C75127FF",
                                              "Tasso ID 14" = "#D58F5CFF",
                                              "Tasso ID 15" = "#7A65A5FF",
                                              "Tasso ID 17" = "#E4AF69FF",
                                              "Tasso ID 18" = "#3B1B53FF",
                                              "Tasso ID 19" = "#CDDEB7FF",
                                              "Tasso ID 20" = "#612A79FF",
                                              "Tasso ID 21" = "#AE1F63FF",
                                              "SAC253053"   = "#E7C76FFF"
                                              ))


```

# Create Objects That Contain The Variables We Wish to Plot in Different Instances

We will be passing plotting functions a list of the variables we want to plot. Bellow are objects that contain either all the variables we want to plot or just variables related to the statistic we are plotting (percent vs mfi, helpful when scaling becomes an issue).

```{r create_variables_to_pass_plotting_functions}

## Create objects to store plotting columns and meta data columns

colnames(data_phen_all)
plot_phen_cols_names <- c("BTLA_Percent_Positive",
                          "CD25_Percent_Positive",
                          "CD27_Percent_Positive",
                          "CD28_Percent_Positive",
                          "CD38_Percent_Positive",
                          "CD69_Percent_Positive",
                          "CD103_Percent_Positive",
                          "CD127_Percent_Positive",
                          "CD137_Percent_Positive",
                          "CD161_Percent_Positive",
                          "CD223_Percent_Positive",
                          "GranzymeB_Percent_Positive",
                          "HLADR_Percent_Positive",
                          "ICOS_Percent_Positive",
                          "KI67_Percent_Positive",
                          "OX40_Percent_Positive",
                          "PD1_Percent_Positive",
                          "TIM3_Percent_Positive")

colnames(data_frequency_all)

plot_freq_all_cols_names <- c("Live_Percent_of_Single_Cells",
                            "CD45_Percent_Positive_of_Live",
                            
                            "B_cells_Total_Percent_of_CD45_Positive",
                            "T_cells_Total_Percent_of_CD45_Positive",
                        
                            "TCRgd_T_cells_Total_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "TCRgd_T_cells_Naive_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "TCRgd_T_cells_TEMRA_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "TCRgd_T_cells_CM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "TCRgd_T_cells_EM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "TCRgd_T_cells_Total_Memory_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",

                            "MAIT_cells_Naive_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "MAIT_T_cells_TEMRA_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "MAIT_T_cells_CM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "MAIT_T_cells_EM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "MAIT_T_cells_Total_Memory_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                        
                            "Treg_cells_Total_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Treg_cells_Naive_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Treg_cells_TEMRA_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Treg_cells_CM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Treg_cells_EM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",

                            "Conventional_CD8_T_cells_Naive_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Conventional_CD8_T_cells_TEMRA_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Conventional_CD8_T_cells_CM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Conventional_CD8_T_cells_EM_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",
                            "Conventional_CD8_T_cells_Total_Memory_Percent_of_Total_T_cells",

                            "TEMRA_Percent_of_MAIT_cells_Total",
                            "CM_Percent_of_MAIT_cells_Total",
                            "EM_Percent_of_MAIT_cells_Total",
                            "Total_Memory_Percent_of_MAIT_cells_Total",

                            "TEMRA_Percent_of_Treg_cells_Total",

                            "Memory_Percent_of_B_cells",         #Added for review comments 2025-12-30 AK
                            "Naive_Percent_of_B_cells")          #Added for review comments 2025-12-30 AK

                            "CD45_Percent_Positive_of_Live",
                            
                            "B_cells_Total_Percent_of_CD45_Positive",
                            "T_cells_Total_Percent_of_CD45_Positive",

```

# Plan Comparisons for Plotting

This section just looks at all experiment groups available in the data so that we can decide which we will plot.

```{r plan_comparisons_for_ggstatplot}

# Assess metadata to plan comparisons

temp <- data_frequency_all %>% filter(Experiment == 3 & Group == "Tasso WB Shipped")
mean(temp$Live_Percent_of_Single_Cells)
sd(temp$Live_Percent_of_Single_Cells)

table(data_phen_all$`Group`, data_phen_all$Population)

## Will run paired comparison tests for all (ggwithinstats) this will use the function ggwithinstats

### (0) Control: Biologic replicate SAC253053 over batches
### (1) Experiment 1: Fresh v Frozen Samples
### (2) Experiment 2: Pilot Frozen
### (3) Experiment 3: Batch 1 and Batch 2

## Will use pheatmaps to generate a heat map for each time point comparing all stim conditions for select populations (Putative MAITs, TCRgd, CD4 Total Memory, and CD8 Total Memory). This tool will report just the means of percentages and can be used to decide which comparisons would be interesting to explore in the compared groups. 

```

# Comparison 3: 2 Batches 16 samples total

```{r comparisons_3_1}
# Comparison 3: 2 Batches 16 samples total
## 16 donors processed in four ways Vein PBMC, Vein WB, Tasso WB, and Tasso WB `Mock` Shipped. All cryopreserved before staining.
## Have an n of 16, will use heatmaps to report means and then will do individual parameter testing

## Comparison 3_1: Heatmaps

# Set up directories
dir.plots_analysis <- file.path(dir.plots,"Comparison_3_1")
dir.create(dir.plots_analysis)

## Population Frequency Heatmap ################################################

# Filter data set for samples only apart of the analysis

data_frequency_all_selected <- data_frequency_all %>%
  filter(Experiment == 3 & Donor != "SAC253053" & Group != "Vein PBMC Pre")

plot_freq_major_cols_names

# Organize mean table

data_frequency_all_selected_means <- data_frequency_all_selected %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  summarise(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)), 
    across(!where(is.numeric), ~ head(.x, 1))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

table(data_frequency_all_selected$Donor, data_frequency_all_selected$Group)

data_frequency_all_selected_means <- data_frequency_all_selected_means %>%
  arrange(Group)

r_names <- paste(data_frequency_all_selected_means$Group)

data_frequency_all_selected_means <- data_frequency_all_selected_means[,c(plot_freq_all_cols_names)]

rownames(data_frequency_all_selected_means) <- r_names

# Organize median table

data_frequency_all_selected_median <- data_frequency_all_selected %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  summarise(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ median(.x, na.rm = TRUE)), 
    across(!where(is.numeric), ~ head(.x, 1))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

table(data_frequency_all_selected$Donor, data_frequency_all_selected$Group)

data_frequency_all_selected_median <- data_frequency_all_selected_median %>%
  arrange(Group)

r_names <- paste(data_frequency_all_selected_median$Group)

data_frequency_all_selected_median <- data_frequency_all_selected_median[,c(plot_freq_all_cols_names)]

rownames(data_frequency_all_selected_median) <- r_names

# Organize 95% confidence interval table

data_frequency_all_selected_ci <- data_frequency_all_selected %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  summarise(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ calculate_ci(.x)), 
    across(!where(is.numeric), ~ head(.x, 1))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

data_frequency_all_selected_ci <- data_frequency_all_selected_ci %>%
  arrange(Group)

data_frequency_all_selected_ci <- data_frequency_all_selected_ci[,c(plot_freq_all_cols_names)]

# Organize standard deviation table

data_frequency_all_selected_sd <- data_frequency_all_selected %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  summarise(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ sd(.x, na.rm = TRUE)), 
    across(!where(is.numeric), ~ head(.x, 1))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

data_frequency_all_selected_sd <- data_frequency_all_selected_sd %>%
  arrange(Group)

data_frequency_all_selected_sd <-data_frequency_all_selected_sd[,c(plot_freq_all_cols_names)]

# Organize IQR table

data_frequency_all_selected_iqr <- data_frequency_all_selected %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  summarise(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ calculate_iqr(.x)), 
    across(!where(is.numeric), ~ head(.x, 1))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

data_frequency_all_selected_iqr <- data_frequency_all_selected_iqr %>%
  arrange(Group)

data_frequency_all_selected_iqr <-data_frequency_all_selected_iqr[,c(plot_freq_all_cols_names)]

# Create tables annotating both mean or meadian, and measures of variance

data_frequency_all_selected_means_and_ci <- matrix(paste(sprintf("%.1f",t(data_frequency_all_selected_means)),
                                                         "\n",
                                                         # " ",
                                                         "[", t(data_frequency_all_selected_ci),"]",
                                                         sep =""), 
                                                   ncol=4)

colnames(data_frequency_all_selected_means_and_ci) <- colnames(t(data_frequency_all_selected_means))
rownames(data_frequency_all_selected_means_and_ci) <- rownames(t(data_frequency_all_selected_means))
data_frequency_all_selected_means_and_ci

data_frequency_all_selected_means_and_sd <- matrix(paste(sprintf("%.1f",t(data_frequency_all_selected_means)),
                                                         "\n",
                                                         # " ",
                                                         "[", sprintf("%.1f",t(data_frequency_all_selected_sd)),"]",
                                                         sep =""), 
                                                   ncol=4)
colnames(data_frequency_all_selected_means_and_sd) <- colnames(t(data_frequency_all_selected_means))
rownames(data_frequency_all_selected_means_and_sd) <- rownames(t(data_frequency_all_selected_means))
data_frequency_all_selected_means_and_sd

data_frequency_all_selected_median_and_iqr <- matrix(paste(sprintf("%.1f",t(data_frequency_all_selected_median)),
                                                         "\n",
                                                         # " ",
                                                         "[", t(data_frequency_all_selected_iqr),"]",
                                                         sep =""), 
                                                   ncol=4)
colnames(data_frequency_all_selected_median_and_iqr) <- colnames(t(data_frequency_all_selected_median))
rownames(data_frequency_all_selected_median_and_iqr) <- rownames(t(data_frequency_all_selected_median))
data_frequency_all_selected_median_and_iqr

data_frequency_all_selected_means <- t(data_frequency_all_selected_means)
data_frequency_all_selected_median <- t(data_frequency_all_selected_median)

# Make population frequency heatmaps and save

## Heatmap mean only

pheatmap_freq_major_sub <- pheatmap(data_frequency_all_selected_means[plot_freq_major_sub_cols_names,],
         main = "Population Frequency Means - T cell Major Cell Types - Shipped Cohort",
         # scale = "column",
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         cluster_rows = FALSE,
         gaps_row = c(1,2,4),
         # gaps_col = c(2,4,6),
         display_numbers = TRUE,
         number_format = "%.1f",
         breaks = seq(0,100, length.out = (100 + 1)),
         # show_rownames = FALSE,
         # show_colnames = FALSE,
         color = viridis(100, direction = 1 ),
         number_color = "white",
         labels_row = str_replace_all(plot_freq_major_sub_cols_names, "_", " "),
         fontsize_number = 10
         )

ggsave(filename = "Population Frequency Means - T cell Major Cell Types - Shipped Cohort.pdf",
       plot = pheatmap_freq_major_sub,
       path = dir.plots_analysis,
       width = 11,
       height = 8)

pheatmap_freq_memory_of_CD4 <- pheatmap(data_frequency_all_selected_means[plot_freq_memory_of_CD4_cols_names,],
         main = "Population Frequency Means - Memory of Conventional CD4 T cells - Shipped Cohort",
         # scale = "column",
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         cluster_rows = FALSE,
         display_numbers = TRUE,
         number_format = "%.1f",
         breaks = seq(0,100, length.out = (100 + 1)),
         # show_rownames = FALSE,
         # show_colnames = FALSE,
         color = viridis(100, direction = 1 ),
         number_color = "white",
         labels_row = c("Naive", "TEMRA", "Central Memory", "Effector Memory", "Total Memory"),
         fontsize_number = 10,
         )

ggsave(filename = "Population Frequency Means - Memory of Conventional CD4 T cells - Shipped Cohort.pdf",
       plot = pheatmap_freq_memory_of_CD4,
       path = dir.plots_analysis,
       width = 10,
       height = 5)

pheatmap_freq_memory_of_CD8 <- pheatmap(data_frequency_all_selected_means[plot_freq_memory_of_CD8_cols_names,],
         main = "Population Frequency Means - Memory of Conventional CD8 T cells - Shipped Cohort",
         # scale = "column",
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         cluster_rows = FALSE,
         display_numbers = TRUE,
         number_format = "%.1f",
         breaks = seq(0,100, length.out = (100 + 1)),
         # show_rownames = FALSE,
         # show_colnames = FALSE,
         color = viridis(100, direction = 1 ),
         number_color = "white",
         labels_row = c("Naive", "TEMRA", "Central Memory", "Effector Memory", "Total Memory"),
         fontsize_number = 10,
         )

ggsave(filename = "Population Frequency Means - Memory of Conventional CD8 T cells - Shipped Cohort.pdf",
       plot = pheatmap_freq_memory_of_CD8,
       path = dir.plots_analysis,
       width = 10,
       height = 5)

pheatmap_freq_memory_of_Treg <- pheatmap(data_frequency_all_selected_means[plot_freq_memory_of_Treg_cols_names,],
         main = "Population Frequency Means - Memory of Treg T cells - Shipped Cohort",
         # scale = "column",
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         cluster_rows = FALSE,
         display_numbers = TRUE,
         number_format = "%.1f",
         breaks = seq(0,100, length.out = (100 + 1)),
         # show_rownames = FALSE,
         # show_colnames = FALSE,
         color = viridis(100, direction = 1 ),
         number_color = "white",
         labels_row = c("Naive", "TEMRA", "Central Memory", "Effector Memory", "Total Memory"),
         fontsize_number = 10,
         )

ggsave(filename = "Population Frequency Means - Memory of Treg T cells - Shipped Cohort.pdf",
       plot = pheatmap_freq_memory_of_Treg,
       path = dir.plots_analysis,
       width = 10,
       height = 5)

pheatmap_freq_memory_of_MAIT <- pheatmap(data_frequency_all_selected_means[plot_freq_memory_of_MAIT_cols_names,],
         main = "Population Frequency Means - Memory of MAIT cells - Shipped Cohort",
         # scale = "column",
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         cluster_rows = FALSE,
         display_numbers = TRUE,
         number_format = "%.1f",
         breaks = seq(0,100, length.out = (100 + 1)),
         # show_rownames = FALSE,
         # show_colnames = FALSE,
         color = viridis(100, direction = 1 ),
         number_color = "white",
         labels_row = c("Naive", "TEMRA", "Central Memory", "Effector Memory", "Total Memory"),
         fontsize_number = 10,
         )

ggsave(filename = "Population Frequency Means - Memory of MAIT cells - Shipped Cohort.pdf",
       plot = pheatmap_freq_memory_of_MAIT,
       path = dir.plots_analysis,
       width = 10,
       height = 5)

pheatmap_freq_memory_of_TCRgd <- pheatmap(data_frequency_all_selected_means[plot_freq_memory_of_TCRgd_cols_names,],
         main = "Population Frequency Means - Memory of TCRgd T cells - Shipped Cohort",
         # scale = "column",
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         cluster_rows = FALSE,
         display_numbers = TRUE,
         number_format = "%.1f",
         breaks = seq(0,100, length.out = (100 + 1)),
         # show_rownames = FALSE,
         # show_colnames = FALSE,
         color = viridis(100, direction = 1 ),
         number_color = "white",
         labels_row = c("Naive", "TEMRA", "Central Memory", "Effector Memory", "Total Memory"),
         fontsize_number = 10,
         )

ggsave(filename = "Population Frequency Means - Memory of TCRgd T cells - Shipped Cohort.pdf",
       plot = pheatmap_freq_memory_of_TCRgd,
       path = dir.plots_analysis,
       width = 10,
       height = 5)
```
