```{r comparisons_4_1}
         color = viridis(100, direction = 1 ),
         number_color = "white",
         labels_row = c("Naive", "TEMRA", "Central Memory", "Effector Memory", "Total Memory"),
         fontsize_number = 10,
         )

ggsave(filename = "Population Frequency Medians and IQR - Memory of TCRgd T cells - Shipped Cohort.pdf",
       plot = pheatmap_freq_memory_of_TCRgd,
       path = dir.plots_analysis,
       width = 10,
       height = 5)


## Phenotyping Frequency Heatmap ###############################################

# Filter data set for samples only apart of the analysis

data_phen_all_selected <- data_phen_all %>%
  filter(Experiment == 3 & Donor != "SAC253053" & Group != "Vein PBMC Pre") %>%
  filter(Donor %in% filtered_tasso_id)

# Start organize mean table

data_phen_all_selected_means <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
  group_by(Population, Group) %>%
  summarise(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)), 
    across(!where(is.numeric), ~ head(.x, 1))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

data_phen_all_selected_means <- data_phen_all_selected_means %>%
  arrange(Population, Group)

# Start organize median table

data_phen_all_selected_median <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
  group_by(Population, Group) %>%
  summarise(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ median(.x, na.rm = TRUE)), 
    across(!where(is.numeric), ~ head(.x, 1))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

data_phen_all_selected_median <- data_phen_all_selected_median %>%
  arrange(Population, Group)

# Start organize 95% confidence interval table

data_phen_all_selected_ci <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
  group_by(Population, Group) %>%
  summarise(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ calculate_ci(.x)), 
    across(!where(is.numeric), ~ head(.x, 1))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

data_phen_all_selected_ci <- data_phen_all_selected_ci %>%
  arrange(Population, Group)

# Start organize standard deviation table

data_phen_all_selected_sd <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
  group_by(Population, Group) %>%
  summarise(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ sd(.x, na.rm = TRUE)), 
    across(!where(is.numeric), ~ head(.x, 1))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

data_phen_all_selected_sd <- data_phen_all_selected_sd %>%
  arrange(Population, Group)

# Start organize iqr table

data_phen_all_selected_iqr <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
  group_by(Population, Group) %>%
  summarise(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ calculate_iqr(.x)), 
    across(!where(is.numeric), ~ head(.x, 1))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

data_phen_all_selected_iqr <- data_phen_all_selected_iqr %>%
  arrange(Population, Group)

# Iterate trough populations for each table, make heatmap, and save

for (i in unique(data_phen_all_selected_means$Population)){
  
  print(i)
  
  # Filter mean table for population and finish organizing
  
  data_phen_all_selected_means_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_means %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  r_names <- paste(data_phen_all_selected_means_pop$Group)
  
  data_phen_all_selected_means_pop <-  data_phen_all_selected_means_pop[,c(plot_phen_cols_names)]
  
  rownames(data_phen_all_selected_means_pop) <- r_names
  
    # Filter median table for population and finish organizing
  
  data_phen_all_selected_median_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_median %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  r_names <- paste(data_phen_all_selected_median_pop$Group)
  
  data_phen_all_selected_median_pop <-  data_phen_all_selected_median_pop[,c(plot_phen_cols_names)]
  
  rownames(data_phen_all_selected_median_pop) <- r_names
  
  # Filter 95% confidence interval table and finish organizing
  
  data_phen_all_selected_ci_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_ci %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  data_phen_all_selected_ci_pop <-data_phen_all_selected_ci_pop[,c(plot_phen_cols_names)]
  
  data_phen_all_selected_means_and_ci_pop <- matrix(paste(sprintf("%.1f",unlist(data_phen_all_selected_means_pop)),
                                                          "\n",
                                                          # " ",
                                                          "[", unlist(data_phen_all_selected_ci_pop),"]",
                                                          sep =""), 
                                                    ncol=length(plot_phen_cols_names))
  
  data_phen_all_selected_means_and_ci_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_means_and_ci_pop
    
  # Filter standard deviation table and finish organizing
  
  data_phen_all_selected_sd_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_sd %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  data_phen_all_selected_sd_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_sd_pop[,c(plot_phen_cols_names)]
  
  data_phen_all_selected_means_and_sd_pop <- matrix(paste(sprintf("%.1f",unlist(data_phen_all_selected_means_pop)),
                                                          "\n",
                                                          # " ",
                                                          "[", sprintf("%.1f",unlist(data_phen_all_selected_sd_pop)),"]",
                                                          sep =""),
                                                    ncol=length(plot_phen_cols_names))
  
  # Filter iqr table and finish organizing
  
  data_phen_all_selected_iqr_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_iqr %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  data_phen_all_selected_iqr_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_iqr_pop[,c(plot_phen_cols_names)]
  
  data_phen_all_selected_median_and_iqr_pop <- matrix(paste(sprintf("%.1f",unlist(data_phen_all_selected_median_pop)),
                                                          "\n",
                                                          # " ",
                                                          "[", unlist(data_phen_all_selected_iqr_pop),"]",
                                                          sep =""), 
                                                    ncol=length(plot_phen_cols_names))
  
  data_phen_all_selected_median_and_iqr_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_median_and_iqr_pop

  # Create a temp variable for number of donors for n
  
  temp <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  temp_n <- length(unique(temp$Donor))
  
  # Make phenotyping frequency heatmaps and save
  
  ## Heatmap mean only
  
  pheatmap_temp <- pheatmap(data_phen_all_selected_means_pop,
         main = paste("Phenotyping Frequency Means - Shipped Cohort", "            ", "Donors - ", temp_n, "            ", "Population - ", i, sep = ""),
         # scale = "column",
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         cluster_rows = FALSE,
         # gaps_row = c(2,4,6),
         # gaps_row = c(13,26,39,52),
         display_numbers = TRUE,
         number_format = "%.1f",
         breaks = seq(0,100, length.out = (100 + 1)),
         # show_rownames = FALSE,
         # show_colnames = FALSE,
         color = viridis(100, direction = 1 ),
         number_color = "white",
         fontsize_number = 10
         )
  
  ggsave(filename = paste("Phenotyping Frequency Means - Shipped Cohort", " ", "Donors - ", temp_n, " ", "Population - ", i, ".pdf",sep = ""),
       plot = pheatmap_temp,
       path = dir.plots_analysis,
       width = 16.5,
       height = 6.375
       )
  
  ## Heatmap mean and standard deviation
  
  pheatmap_temp <- pheatmap(data_phen_all_selected_means_pop,
                            main = paste("Phenotyping Frequency Means and SD - Shipped Cohort", "            ", "Donors - ", temp_n, "            ", "Population - ", i, sep = ""),
                            # scale = "column",
                            cluster_cols = FALSE,
                            cluster_rows = FALSE,
                            # gaps_row = c(2,4,6),
                            # gaps_row = c(13,26,39,52),
                            display_numbers = data_phen_all_selected_means_and_sd_pop,
                            number_format = "%.1f",
                            breaks = seq(0,100, length.out = (100 + 1)),
                            # show_rownames = FALSE,
                            # show_colnames = FALSE,
                            color = viridis(100, direction = 1 ),
                            number_color = "white",
                            fontsize_number = 10
                            )
  
  ggsave(filename = paste("Phenotyping Frequency Means and SD - Shipped Cohort", " ", "Donors - ", temp_n, " ", "Population - ", i, ".pdf",sep = ""),
         plot = pheatmap_temp,
         path = dir.plots_analysis,
         width = 16.5,
         height = 6.375
         )
  
  ## Heatmap mean and 95% confidence interval
  
  pheatmap_temp <- pheatmap(data_phen_all_selected_means_pop,
                            main = paste("Phenotyping Frequency Means and CI - Shipped Cohort", "            ", "Donors - ", temp_n, "            ", "Population- ", i, sep = ""),
                            # scale = "column",
                            cluster_cols = FALSE,
                            cluster_rows = FALSE,
                            # gaps_row = c(2,4,6),
                            # gaps_row = c(13,26,39,52),
                            display_numbers = data_phen_all_selected_means_and_ci_pop,
                            number_format = "%.1f",
                            breaks = seq(0,100, length.out = (100 + 1)),
                            # show_rownames = FALSE,
                            # show_colnames = FALSE,
                            color = viridis(100, direction = 1 ),
                            number_color = "white",
                            fontsize_number = 9
                            )
  
  ggsave(filename = paste("Phenotyping Frequency Means and CI - Shipped Cohort", " ", "Donors - ", temp_n, " ", "Population - ", i, ".pdf",sep = ""),
         plot = pheatmap_temp,
         path = dir.plots_analysis,
         width = 16.5,
         height = 6.375
         )
  
    ## Heatmap median and iqr table
  
  pheatmap_temp <- pheatmap(data_phen_all_selected_median_pop,
                            main = paste("Phenotyping Frequency Medians and IQR - Shipped Cohort", "            ", "Donors - ", temp_n, "            ", "Population- ", i, sep = ""),
                            # scale = "column",
                            cluster_cols = FALSE,
                            cluster_rows = FALSE,
                            # gaps_row = c(2,4,6),
                            # gaps_row = c(13,26,39,52),
                            display_numbers = data_phen_all_selected_median_and_iqr_pop,
                            number_format = "%.1f",
                            breaks = seq(0,100, length.out = (100 + 1)),
                            # show_rownames = FALSE,
                            # show_colnames = FALSE,
                            color = viridis(100, direction = 1 ),
                            number_color = "white",
                            fontsize_number = 10
                            )
  
  ggsave(filename = paste("Phenotyping Frequency Medians and IQR - Shipped Cohort", " ", "Donors - ", temp_n, " ", "Population - ", i, ".pdf",sep = ""),
         plot = pheatmap_temp,
         path = dir.plots_analysis,
         width = 16.5,
         height = 6.375
         )
  
}

write.csv(data_frequency_all_selected,
          file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, "data_frequency_all_selected.csv"))

write.csv(data_phen_all_selected,
          file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, "data_phen_all_selected.csv"))

```

```{r comparisons_4_2}

## Comparison 4_2: Individual testing using ggstatplots

## Population Frequency ggstatplots

dir.plots_analysis <- file.path(dir.plots,"Comparison_4_2")
dir.create(dir.plots_analysis)

data_frequency_all_selected <- data_frequency_all %>%
  filter(Experiment == 3 & Donor != "SAC253053" & Group != "Vein PBMC Pre") %>%
  filter(Donor %in% filtered_tasso_id)

table(data_frequency_all_selected$Donor, data_frequency_all_selected$Group)

data.plot <- data_frequency_all_selected

current_plot_cols_names <- plot_freq_all_cols_names

plotlist <- purrr::pmap(
  .l = list(
    data = list(data.plot),
    x = "Group",
    y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
    title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, sep="")),
    type = "nonparametric",
    plot.type = "box",
    pairwise.display = "none",
    package = "ggsci",
    palette = "default_igv",
    point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
    centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
    violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
    ggplot.component = list(list(
      geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
      ylim(0,NA)
      )
    )),
  .f = ggwithinstats
  )
  
plotlist_with_stats <- purrr::pmap(
  .l = list(
    data = list(data.plot),
    x = "Group",
    y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
    title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, sep="")),
    type = "nonparametric",
    plot.type = "box",
    pairwise.display = "all",
    package = "ggsci",
    palette = "default_igv",
    point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
    centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
    violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
    ggplot.component = list(list(
      geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
      ylim(0,NA)
      )
    )),
  .f = ggwithinstats
  )
  
names(plotlist) <- current_plot_cols_names
names(plotlist_with_stats) <- current_plot_cols_names

stat_list <- lapply(plotlist_with_stats, extract_stats)

dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep="")))
dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""), "stat_tables"))
dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""), "plots"))

for (i in names(stat_list)){
  print(i)
  temp <- dplyr::full_join(stat_list[[i]]$subtitle_data, stat_list[[i]]$pairwise_comparisons_data)
  write_csv(temp,
            file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis,
                             paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""),
                             "stat_tables",
                             paste(i, ".csv", sep = ""))
  )
}
  
lapply(names(plotlist),
       function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(x, ".pdf", sep=""),
                         path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""), "plots"),
                         height = 8,
                         width = 12,
                         plot = plotlist[[x]]))

lapply(names(plotlist_with_stats),
       function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(x, "_with_stats.pdf", sep=""),
                         path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""), "plots"),
                         height = 8,
                         width = 12,
                         plot = plotlist_with_stats[[x]]))

## Phenotyping Frequency ggstatplots

data_phen_all_selected <- data_phen_all %>%
  filter(Experiment == 3 & Donor != "SAC253053" & Group != "Vein PBMC Pre") %>%
  filter(Donor %in% filtered_tasso_id)

table(data_phen_all_selected$Donor, data_phen_all_selected$Group)

plot_cols_names <- c(plot_phen_cols_names, "Count")

for (i in unique(data_phen_all_selected$Population)){
  print(i)
  
  data.plot <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  current_plot_cols_names <- plot_cols_names[plot_cols_names %notin% plot_cols_names[sapply(data.plot[plot_cols_names], sum_rm_na) == 0]] #removes columns where the selected samples have no values for a parameter
  
    current_plot_cols_names <- plot_cols_names[plot_cols_names %notin% plot_cols_names[sapply(data.plot[plot_cols_names], sd) == 0]] #removes columns where there is no differences, messes up ggstatplot otherwise
  
  plotlist <- purrr::pmap(
    .l = list(
      data = list(data.plot),
      x = "Group",
      y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
      title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, "      Population: ", i, sep="")),
      type = "nonparametric",
      plot.type = "box",
      pairwise.display = "none",
      package = "ggsci",
      palette = "default_igv",
      silent = TRUE,
      point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
      centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
      violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
      ggplot.component = list(list(
      geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
      ylim(0,NA)
      )
    )),
    .f = ggwithinstats
  )
  
  plotlist_with_stats <- purrr::pmap(
    .l = list(
      data = list(data.plot),
      x = "Group",
      y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
      title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, "      Population: ", i, sep="")),
      type = "nonparametric",
      plot.type = "box",
      pairwise.display = "all",
      package = "ggsci",
      palette = "default_igv",
      silent = TRUE,
      point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
      centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
      violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
      ggplot.component = list(list(
      geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
      ylim(0,NA)
      )
    )),
    .f = ggwithinstats
  )
  
  names(plotlist) <- current_plot_cols_names
  names(plotlist_with_stats) <- current_plot_cols_names
  
  stat_list <- lapply(plotlist_with_stats, extract_stats)
  
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep="")))
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""),"stat_tables"))
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""),"plots"))
  
  for (x in names(stat_list)){
  # print(x)
  temp <- dplyr::full_join(stat_list[[x]]$subtitle_data, stat_list[[x]]$pairwise_comparisons_data)
  write_csv(temp,
            file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis,
                             paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""),
                             "stat_tables",
                             paste(i, " ", x, ".csv", sep = ""))
  )
}
  
  lapply(names(plotlist),
         function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(i, " ", x, ".pdf", 
                                            sep=""),
                           path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"),
                           height = 8,
                           width = 12, 
                           plot = plotlist[[x]]))
  
  lapply(names(plotlist),
         function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(i, " ", x, "_with_stats.pdf",
                                            sep=""),
                           path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"),
                           height = 8,
                           width = 12,
                           plot = plotlist_with_stats[[x]]))
}

write.csv(data_frequency_all_selected,
          file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, "data_frequency_all_selected.csv"))

write.csv(data_phen_all_selected,
          file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, "data_phen_all_selected.csv"))

```

```{r comparisons_4_1_F}

## Comparison 4_1_F: Heatmaps with cell filter applied to phenotyping outputs

# Set up directories

dir.plots_analysis <- file.path(dir.plots,"Comparison_4_1_F")
dir.create(dir.plots_analysis)

## Phenotyping Frequency Filtered Heatmap #######################################

# Filter data set for samples only apart of the analysis

data_phen_all_selected <- data_phen_all %>%
  filter(Experiment == 3 & Donor != "SAC253053" & Group != "Vein PBMC Pre") %>%
  filter(Donor %in% filtered_tasso_id)

# Filter out low cell count sets

sets_to_remove <- unique(data_phen_all_selected[data_phen_all_selected$Filter == TRUE,]$Donor_Population_Date)

data_phen_all_selected <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
  filter(Donor_Population_Date %notin% sets_to_remove)

table(data_phen_all_selected$Donor, data_phen_all_selected$Group)

table(data_phen_all_selected$Population, data_phen_all_selected$Group)

# Start organize mean table

data_phen_all_selected_means <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
  group_by(Population, Group) %>%
  summarise(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)), 
    across(!where(is.numeric), ~ head(.x, 1))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

data_phen_all_selected_means <- data_phen_all_selected_means %>%
  arrange(Population, Group)

# Start organize median table

data_phen_all_selected_median <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
  group_by(Population, Group) %>%
  summarise(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ median(.x, na.rm = TRUE)), 
    across(!where(is.numeric), ~ head(.x, 1))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

data_phen_all_selected_median <- data_phen_all_selected_median %>%
  arrange(Population, Group)

# Start organize 95% confidence interval table

data_phen_all_selected_ci <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
  group_by(Population, Group) %>%
  summarise(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ calculate_ci(.x)), 
    across(!where(is.numeric), ~ head(.x, 1))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

data_phen_all_selected_ci <- data_phen_all_selected_ci %>%
  arrange(Population, Group)

# Start organize standard deviation table

data_phen_all_selected_sd <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
  group_by(Population, Group) %>%
  summarise(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ sd(.x, na.rm = TRUE)), 
    across(!where(is.numeric), ~ head(.x, 1))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

data_phen_all_selected_sd <- data_phen_all_selected_sd %>%
  arrange(Population, Group)

# Start organize iqr table

data_phen_all_selected_iqr <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
  group_by(Population, Group) %>%
  summarise(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ calculate_iqr(.x)), 
    across(!where(is.numeric), ~ head(.x, 1))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

data_phen_all_selected_iqr <- data_phen_all_selected_iqr %>%
  arrange(Population, Group)

# Iterate trough populations for each table, make heatmap, and save

for (i in unique(data_phen_all_selected_means$Population)){
  
  print(i)
  
  # Filter mean table for population and finish organizing
  
  data_phen_all_selected_means_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_means %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  r_names <- paste(data_phen_all_selected_means_pop$Group)
  
  data_phen_all_selected_means_pop <-  data_phen_all_selected_means_pop[,c(plot_phen_cols_names)]
  
  rownames(data_phen_all_selected_means_pop) <- r_names
  
    # Filter median table for population and finish organizing
  
  data_phen_all_selected_median_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_median %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  r_names <- paste(data_phen_all_selected_median_pop$Group)
  
  data_phen_all_selected_median_pop <-  data_phen_all_selected_median_pop[,c(plot_phen_cols_names)]
  
  rownames(data_phen_all_selected_median_pop) <- r_names
  
  # Filter 95% confidence interval table and finish organizing
  
  data_phen_all_selected_ci_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_ci %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  data_phen_all_selected_ci_pop <-data_phen_all_selected_ci_pop[,c(plot_phen_cols_names)]
  
  data_phen_all_selected_means_and_ci_pop <- matrix(paste(sprintf("%.1f",unlist(data_phen_all_selected_means_pop)),
                                                          "\n",
                                                          # " ",
                                                          "[", unlist(data_phen_all_selected_ci_pop),"]",
                                                          sep =""), 
                                                    ncol=length(plot_phen_cols_names))
  
  data_phen_all_selected_means_and_ci_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_means_and_ci_pop
    
  # Filter standard deviation table and finish organizing
  
  data_phen_all_selected_sd_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_sd %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  data_phen_all_selected_sd_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_sd_pop[,c(plot_phen_cols_names)]
  
  data_phen_all_selected_means_and_sd_pop <- matrix(paste(sprintf("%.1f",unlist(data_phen_all_selected_means_pop)),
                                                          "\n",
                                                          # " ",
                                                          "[", sprintf("%.1f",unlist(data_phen_all_selected_sd_pop)),"]",
                                                          sep =""),
                                                    ncol=length(plot_phen_cols_names))
  
  # Filter iqr table and finish organizing
  
  data_phen_all_selected_iqr_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_iqr %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  data_phen_all_selected_iqr_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_iqr_pop[,c(plot_phen_cols_names)]
  
  data_phen_all_selected_median_and_iqr_pop <- matrix(paste(sprintf("%.1f",unlist(data_phen_all_selected_median_pop)),
                                                          "\n",
                                                          # " ",
                                                          "[", unlist(data_phen_all_selected_iqr_pop),"]",
                                                          sep =""), 
                                                    ncol=length(plot_phen_cols_names))
  
  data_phen_all_selected_median_and_iqr_pop <- data_phen_all_selected_median_and_iqr_pop

  # Create a temp variable for number of donors for n
  
  temp <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  temp_n <- length(unique(temp$Donor))
  
  # Make phenotyping frequency heatmaps and save
  
  ## Heatmap mean only
  
  pheatmap_temp <- pheatmap(data_phen_all_selected_means_pop,
         main = paste("Phenotyping Frequency Means - Shipped Cohort", "            ", "Donors - ", temp_n, "            ", "Population - ", i, sep = ""),
         # scale = "column",
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         cluster_rows = FALSE,
         # gaps_row = c(2,4,6),
         # gaps_row = c(13,26,39,52),
         display_numbers = TRUE,
         number_format = "%.1f",
         breaks = seq(0,100, length.out = (100 + 1)),
         # show_rownames = FALSE,
         # show_colnames = FALSE,
         color = viridis(100, direction = 1 ),
         number_color = "white",
         fontsize_number = 10
         )
  
  ggsave(filename = paste("Phenotyping Frequency Means - Shipped Cohort", " ", "Donors - ", temp_n, " ", "Population - ", i, ".pdf",sep = ""),
       plot = pheatmap_temp,
       path = dir.plots_analysis,
       width = 16.5,
       height = 6.375
       )
  
  ## Heatmap mean and standard deviation
  
  pheatmap_temp <- pheatmap(data_phen_all_selected_means_pop,
                            main = paste("Phenotyping Frequency Means and SD - Shipped Cohort", "            ", "Donors - ", temp_n, "            ", "Population - ", i, sep = ""),
                            # scale = "column",
                            cluster_cols = FALSE,
                            cluster_rows = FALSE,
                            # gaps_row = c(2,4,6),
                            # gaps_row = c(13,26,39,52),
                            display_numbers = data_phen_all_selected_means_and_sd_pop,
                            number_format = "%.1f",
                            breaks = seq(0,100, length.out = (100 + 1)),
                            # show_rownames = FALSE,
                            # show_colnames = FALSE,
                            color = viridis(100, direction = 1 ),
                            number_color = "white",
                            fontsize_number = 10
                            )
  
  ggsave(filename = paste("Phenotyping Frequency Means and SD - Shipped Cohort", " ", "Donors - ", temp_n, " ", "Population - ", i, ".pdf",sep = ""),
         plot = pheatmap_temp,
         path = dir.plots_analysis,
         width = 16.5,
         height = 6.375
         )
  
  ## Heatmap mean and 95% confidence interval
  
  pheatmap_temp <- pheatmap(data_phen_all_selected_means_pop,
                            main = paste("Phenotyping Frequency Means and CI - Shipped Cohort", "            ", "Donors - ", temp_n, "            ", "Population- ", i, sep = ""),
                            # scale = "column",
                            cluster_cols = FALSE,
                            cluster_rows = FALSE,
                            # gaps_row = c(2,4,6),
                            # gaps_row = c(13,26,39,52),
                            display_numbers = data_phen_all_selected_means_and_ci_pop,
                            number_format = "%.1f",
                            breaks = seq(0,100, length.out = (100 + 1)),
                            # show_rownames = FALSE,
                            # show_colnames = FALSE,
                            color = viridis(100, direction = 1 ),
                            number_color = "white",
                            fontsize_number = 9
                            )
  
  ggsave(filename = paste("Phenotyping Frequency Means and CI - Shipped Cohort", " ", "Donors - ", temp_n, " ", "Population - ", i, ".pdf",sep = ""),
         plot = pheatmap_temp,
         path = dir.plots_analysis,
         width = 16.5,
         height = 6.375
         )
  
    ## Heatmap median and iqr table
  
  pheatmap_temp <- pheatmap(data_phen_all_selected_median_pop,
                            main = paste("Phenotyping Frequency Medians and IQR - Shipped Cohort", "            ", "Donors - ", temp_n, "            ", "Population- ", i, sep = ""),
                            # scale = "column",
                            cluster_cols = FALSE,
                            cluster_rows = FALSE,
                            # gaps_row = c(2,4,6),
                            # gaps_row = c(13,26,39,52),
                            display_numbers = data_phen_all_selected_median_and_iqr_pop,
                            number_format = "%.1f",
                            breaks = seq(0,100, length.out = (100 + 1)),
                            # show_rownames = FALSE,
                            # show_colnames = FALSE,
                            color = viridis(100, direction = 1 ),
                            number_color = "white",
                            fontsize_number = 10
                            )
  
  ggsave(filename = paste("Phenotyping Frequency Medians and IQR - Shipped Cohort", " ", "Donors - ", temp_n, " ", "Population - ", i, ".pdf",sep = ""),
         plot = pheatmap_temp,
         path = dir.plots_analysis,
         width = 16.5,
         height = 6.375
         )
  
}

write.csv(data_frequency_all_selected,
          file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, "data_frequency_all_selected.csv"))

write.csv(data_phen_all_selected,
          file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, "data_phen_all_selected.csv"))

```

```{r comparisons_4_2_F}

## Comparison 4_2_F: Individual testing using ggstatplots with cell filter applied to phenotyping outputs

## Phenotyping Frequency ggstatplots

dir.plots_analysis <- file.path(dir.plots,"Comparison_4_2_F")
dir.create(dir.plots_analysis)

data_phen_all_selected <- data_phen_all %>%
  filter(Experiment == 3 & Donor != "SAC253053" & Group != "Vein PBMC Pre") %>%
  filter(Donor %in% filtered_tasso_id)

### Filter out low cell count sets

sets_to_remove <- unique(data_phen_all_selected[data_phen_all_selected$Filter == TRUE,]$Donor_Population_Date)

data_phen_all_selected <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
  filter(Donor_Population_Date %notin% sets_to_remove)

### Filter out low cell count sets

table(data_phen_all_selected$Donor, data_phen_all_selected$Group)
table(data_phen_all_selected$Population, data_phen_all_selected$Group)

plot_cols_names <- c(plot_phen_cols_names, "Count")

for (i in unique(data_phen_all_selected$Population)){
  print(i)
  
  data.plot <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  current_plot_cols_names <- plot_cols_names[plot_cols_names %notin% plot_cols_names[sapply(data.plot[plot_cols_names], sum_rm_na) == 0]] #removes columns where the selected samples have no values for a parameter
  
    current_plot_cols_names <- plot_cols_names[plot_cols_names %notin% plot_cols_names[sapply(data.plot[plot_cols_names], sd) == 0]] #removes columns where there is no differences, messes up ggstatplot otherwise
  
  plotlist <- purrr::pmap(
    .l = list(
      data = list(data.plot),
      x = "Group",
      y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
      title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, "      Population: ", i,  " Filtered",sep="")),
      type = "nonparametric",
      plot.type = "box",
      pairwise.display = "none",
      package = "ggsci",
      palette = "default_igv",
      silent = TRUE,
      point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
      centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
      violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
      ggplot.component = list(list(
      geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
      ylim(0,NA)
      )
    )),
    .f = ggwithinstats
  )
  
  plotlist_with_stats <- purrr::pmap(
    .l = list(
      data = list(data.plot),
      x = "Group",
      y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
      title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, "      Population: ", i,  " Filtered", sep="")),
      type = "nonparametric",
      plot.type = "box",
      pairwise.display = "all",
      package = "ggsci",
      palette = "default_igv",
      silent = TRUE,
      point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
      centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
      violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
      ggplot.component = list(list(
      geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
      ylim(0,NA)
      )
    )),
    .f = ggwithinstats
  )
  
  names(plotlist) <- current_plot_cols_names
  names(plotlist_with_stats) <- current_plot_cols_names
  
  stat_list <- lapply(plotlist_with_stats, extract_stats)
  
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep="")))
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "stat_tables"))
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"))
  
  for (x in names(stat_list)){
    # print(x)
    temp <- dplyr::full_join(stat_list[[x]]$subtitle_data, stat_list[[x]]$pairwise_comparisons_data)
    write_csv(temp,
              file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis,
                               paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""),
                               "stat_tables",
                               paste(i, " ", x, "Filtered.csv", sep = ""))
              )
    }
  
  lapply(names(plotlist),
         function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(i, " ", x, "Filtered.pdf", 
                                            sep=""),
                           path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"),
                           height = 8,
                           width = 12, 
                           plot = plotlist[[x]]))
  
  lapply(names(plotlist),
         function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(i, " ", x, "_with_stats_Filtered.pdf",
                                            sep=""),
                           path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"),
                           height = 8,
                           width = 12,
                           plot = plotlist_with_stats[[x]]))
}

write.csv(data_phen_all_selected,
          file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, "data_phen_all_selected.csv"))

```

```{r comparisons_4_2_Vein_WB_v_Tasso_WB}

## Comparison 4_2: Individual testing using ggstatplots

## Population Frequency ggstatplots

dir.plots_analysis <- file.path(dir.plots,"Comparison_4_2_Vein_WB_v_Tasso_WB")
dir.create(dir.plots_analysis)

data_frequency_all_selected <- data_frequency_all %>%
  filter(Experiment == 3 & Donor != "SAC253053" & Group != "Vein PBMC Pre") %>%
  filter(Group == "Vein WB" | Group == "Tasso WB") %>%
  filter(Donor %in% filtered_tasso_id)

table(data_frequency_all_selected$Donor, data_frequency_all_selected$Group)

data.plot <- data_frequency_all_selected

current_plot_cols_names <- plot_freq_all_cols_names

# plotlist <- purrr::pmap(
#   .l = list(
#     data = list(data.plot),
#     x = "Group",
#     y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
#     title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, sep="")),
#     type = "nonparametric",
#     plot.type = "box",
#     pairwise.display = "none",
#     package = "ggsci",
#     palette = "default_igv",
#     point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
#     centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
#     violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
#     ggplot.component = list(list(
#       geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
#       ylim(0,NA)
#       )
#     )),
#   .f = ggwithinstats
#   )
  
plotlist_with_stats <- purrr::pmap(
  .l = list(
    data = list(data.plot),
    x = "Group",
    y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
    title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, sep="")),
    type = "nonparametric",
    plot.type = "box",
    pairwise.display = "all",
    package = "ggsci",
    palette = "default_igv",
    point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
    centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
    violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
    ggplot.component = list(list(
      geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
      ylim(0,100)
      )
    )),
  .f = ggwithinstats
  )
  
# names(plotlist) <- current_plot_cols_names
names(plotlist_with_stats) <- current_plot_cols_names

stat_list <- lapply(plotlist_with_stats, extract_stats)

dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep="")))
dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""), "stat_tables"))
dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""), "plots"))

for (i in names(stat_list)){
  print(i)
  temp <- stat_list[[i]]$subtitle_data
  write_csv(temp,
            file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis,
                             paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""),
                             "stat_tables",
                             paste(i, ".csv", sep = ""))
  )
}
  
# lapply(names(plotlist),
#        function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(x, ".pdf", sep=""),
#                          path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""), "plots"),
#                          height = 8,
#                          width = 6,
#                          plot = plotlist[[x]]))

lapply(names(plotlist_with_stats),
       function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(x, "_with_stats.pdf", sep=""),
                         path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""), "plots"),
                         height = 8,
                         width = 6,
                         plot = plotlist_with_stats[[x]]))

## Phenotyping Frequency ggstatplots

data_phen_all_selected <- data_phen_all %>%
  filter(Experiment == 3 & Donor != "SAC253053" & Group != "Vein PBMC Pre") %>%
  filter(Group == "Vein WB" | Group == "Tasso WB") %>%
  filter(Donor %in% filtered_tasso_id)

table(data_phen_all_selected$Donor, data_phen_all_selected$Group)

plot_cols_names <- c(plot_phen_cols_names, "Count")

for (i in unique(data_phen_all_selected$Population)){
  print(i)
  
  data.plot <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  current_plot_cols_names <- plot_cols_names[plot_cols_names %notin% plot_cols_names[sapply(data.plot[plot_cols_names], sum_rm_na) == 0]] #removes columns where the selected samples have no values for a parameter
  
    current_plot_cols_names <- plot_cols_names[plot_cols_names %notin% plot_cols_names[sapply(data.plot[plot_cols_names], sd) == 0]] #removes columns where there is no differences, messes up ggstatplot otherwise
  
  # plotlist <- purrr::pmap(
  #   .l = list(
  #     data = list(data.plot),
  #     x = "Group",
  #     y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
  #     title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, "      Population: ", i, sep="")),
  #     type = "nonparametric",
  #     plot.type = "box",
  #     pairwise.display = "none",
  #     package = "ggsci",
  #     palette = "default_igv",
  #     silent = TRUE,
  #     point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
  #     centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
  #     violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
  #     ggplot.component = list(list(
  #     geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
  #     ylim(0,NA)
  #     )
  #   )),
  #   .f = ggwithinstats
  # )
  
  plotlist_with_stats <- purrr::pmap(
    .l = list(
      data = list(data.plot),
      x = "Group",
      y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
      title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, "      Population: ", i, sep="")),
      type = "nonparametric",
      plot.type = "box",
      pairwise.display = "all",
      package = "ggsci",
      palette = "default_igv",
      silent = TRUE,
      point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
      centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
      violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
      ggplot.component = list(list(
      geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
      ylim(0,100)
      )
    )),
    .f = ggwithinstats
  )
  
  # names(plotlist) <- current_plot_cols_names
  names(plotlist_with_stats) <- current_plot_cols_names
  
  stat_list <- lapply(plotlist_with_stats, extract_stats)
  
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep="")))
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""),"stat_tables"))
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""),"plots"))
  
  for (x in names(stat_list)){
  # print(x)
  temp <- stat_list[[x]]$subtitle_data
  write_csv(temp,
            file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis,
                             paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""),
                             "stat_tables",
                             paste(i, " ", x, ".csv", sep = ""))
  )
}
  
  # lapply(names(plotlist),
  #        function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(i, " ", x, ".pdf", 
  #                                           sep=""),
  #                          path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"),
  #                          height = 8,
  #                          width = 6, 
  #                          plot = plotlist[[x]]))
  
  lapply(names(plotlist_with_stats),
         function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(i, " ", x, "_with_stats.pdf",
                                            sep=""),
                           path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"),
                           height = 8,
                           width = 6,
                           plot = plotlist_with_stats[[x]]))
}

write.csv(data_frequency_all_selected,
          file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, "data_frequency_all_selected.csv"))

write.csv(data_phen_all_selected,
          file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, "data_phen_all_selected.csv"))
```

```{r comparisons_4_2_Vein_PBMC_v_Vein_WB}

## Comparison 4_2: Individual testing using ggstatplots

## Population Frequency ggstatplots

dir.plots_analysis <- file.path(dir.plots,"Comparison_4_2_Vein_PBMC_v_Vein_WB")
dir.create(dir.plots_analysis)

data_frequency_all_selected <- data_frequency_all %>%
  filter(Experiment == 3 & Donor != "SAC253053" & Group != "Vein PBMC Pre") %>%
  filter(Group == "Vein PBMC" | Group == "Vein WB") %>%
  filter(Donor %in% filtered_tasso_id)

current_plot_cols_names <- plot_freq_all_cols_names

# plotlist <- purrr::pmap(

#   )
  
plotlist_with_stats <- purrr::pmap(
  .l = list(
    data = list(data.plot),
    x = "Group",
    y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
    title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, sep="")),
    type = "nonparametric",
    plot.type = "box",
    pairwise.display = "all",
    package = "ggsci",
    palette = "default_igv",
    point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
    centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
    violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
    ggplot.component = list(list(
      geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
      ylim(0,100)
      )
    )),
  .f = ggwithinstats
  )
  
# names(plotlist) <- current_plot_cols_names

dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep="")))

for (i in names(stat_list)){
  print(i)
  temp <- stat_list[[i]]$subtitle_data
  write_csv(temp,
            file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis,
                             paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""),
                             "stat_tables",
                             paste(i, ".csv", sep = ""))
  )
}
  
# lapply(names(plotlist),
#        function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(x, ".pdf", sep=""),
#                          path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""), "plots"),
#                          height = 8,
#                          width = 6,
#                          plot = plotlist[[x]]))

lapply(names(plotlist_with_stats),
       function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(x, "_with_stats.pdf", sep=""),
                         path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""), "plots"),
                         height = 8,
                         width = 6,
                         plot = plotlist_with_stats[[x]]))

## Phenotyping Frequency ggstatplots

data_phen_all_selected <- data_phen_all %>%
  filter(Experiment == 3 & Donor != "SAC253053" & Group != "Vein PBMC Pre") %>%
  filter(Group == "Vein PBMC" | Group == "Vein WB") %>%
  filter(Donor %in% filtered_tasso_id)

table(data_phen_all_selected$Donor, data_phen_all_selected$Group)

plot_cols_names <- c(plot_phen_cols_names, "Count")

for (i in unique(data_phen_all_selected$Population)){
  print(i)
  
  data.plot <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  current_plot_cols_names <- plot_cols_names[plot_cols_names %notin% plot_cols_names[sapply(data.plot[plot_cols_names], sum_rm_na) == 0]] #removes columns where the selected samples have no values for a parameter
  
    current_plot_cols_names <- plot_cols_names[plot_cols_names %notin% plot_cols_names[sapply(data.plot[plot_cols_names], sd) == 0]] #removes columns where there is no differences, messes up ggstatplot otherwise
  
  # plotlist <- purrr::pmap(
  #   .l = list(
  #     data = list(data.plot),
  #     x = "Group",
  #     y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
  #     title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, "      Population: ", i, sep="")),
  #     type = "nonparametric",
  #     plot.type = "box",
  #     pairwise.display = "none",
  #     package = "ggsci",
  #     palette = "default_igv",
  #     silent = TRUE,
  #     point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
  #     centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
  #     violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
  #     ggplot.component = list(list(
  #     geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
  #     ylim(0,NA)
  #     )
  #   )),
  #   .f = ggwithinstats
  # )
  
  plotlist_with_stats <- purrr::pmap(
    .l = list(
      data = list(data.plot),
      x = "Group",
      y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
      title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, "      Population: ", i, sep="")),
      type = "nonparametric",
      plot.type = "box",
      pairwise.display = "all",
      package = "ggsci",
      palette = "default_igv",
      silent = TRUE,
      point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
      centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
      violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
      ggplot.component = list(list(
      geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
      ylim(0,100)
      )
    )),
    .f = ggwithinstats
  )
  
  # names(plotlist) <- current_plot_cols_names
  names(plotlist_with_stats) <- current_plot_cols_names
  
  stat_list <- lapply(plotlist_with_stats, extract_stats)
  
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep="")))
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""),"stat_tables"))
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""),"plots"))
  
  for (x in names(stat_list)){
  # print(x)
  temp <- stat_list[[x]]$subtitle_data
  write_csv(temp,
            file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis,
                             paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""),
                             "stat_tables",
                             paste(i, " ", x, ".csv", sep = ""))
  )
}
  
  # lapply(names(plotlist),
  #        function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(i, " ", x, ".pdf", 
  #                                           sep=""),
  #                          path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"),
  #                          height = 8,
  #                          width = 6, 
  #                          plot = plotlist[[x]]))
  
  lapply(names(plotlist_with_stats),
         function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(i, " ", x, "_with_stats.pdf",
                                            sep=""),
                           path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"),
                           height = 8,
                           width = 6,
                           plot = plotlist_with_stats[[x]]))
}

write.csv(data_frequency_all_selected,
          file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, "data_frequency_all_selected.csv"))

write.csv(data_phen_all_selected,
          file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, "data_phen_all_selected.csv"))

```

```{r comparisons_4_2_Tasso_WB_v_Tasso_WB_Shipped}

## Comparison 4_2: Individual testing using ggstatplots

## Population Frequency ggstatplots

dir.plots_analysis <- file.path(dir.plots,"Comparison_4_2_Tasso_WB_v_Tasso_WB_Shipped")
dir.create(dir.plots_analysis)

data_frequency_all_selected <- data_frequency_all %>%
  filter(Experiment == 3 & Donor != "SAC253053" & Group != "Vein PBMC Pre") %>%
  filter(Group == "Tasso WB" | Group == "Tasso WB Shipped") %>%
  filter(Donor %in% filtered_tasso_id)

table(data_frequency_all_selected$Donor, data_frequency_all_selected$Group)

data.plot <- data_frequency_all_selected

current_plot_cols_names <- plot_freq_all_cols_names

# plotlist <- purrr::pmap(
#   .l = list(
#     data = list(data.plot),
#     x = "Group",
#     y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
#     title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, sep="")),
#     type = "nonparametric",
#     plot.type = "box",
#     pairwise.display = "none",
#     package = "ggsci",
#     palette = "default_igv",
#     point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
#     centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
#     violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
#     ggplot.component = list(list(
#       geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
#       ylim(0,NA)
#       )
#     )),
#   .f = ggwithinstats
#   )
  
plotlist_with_stats <- purrr::pmap(
  .l = list(
    data = list(data.plot),
    x = "Group",
    y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
    title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, sep="")),
    type = "nonparametric",
    plot.type = "box",
    pairwise.display = "all",
    package = "ggsci",
    palette = "default_igv",
    point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
    centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
    violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
    ggplot.component = list(list(
      geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
      ylim(0,100)
      )
    )),
  .f = ggwithinstats
  )
  
# names(plotlist) <- current_plot_cols_names
names(plotlist_with_stats) <- current_plot_cols_names

stat_list <- lapply(plotlist_with_stats, extract_stats)

dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep="")))
dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""), "stat_tables"))
dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""), "plots"))

for (i in names(stat_list)){
  print(i)
  temp <- stat_list[[i]]$subtitle_data
  write_csv(temp,
            file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis,
                             paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""),
                             "stat_tables",
                             paste(i, ".csv", sep = ""))
  )
}
  
# lapply(names(plotlist),
#        function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(x, ".pdf", sep=""),
#                          path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""), "plots"),
#                          height = 8,
#                          width = 6,
#                          plot = plotlist[[x]]))

lapply(names(plotlist_with_stats),
       function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(x, "_with_stats.pdf", sep=""),
                         path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", "population frequencies", sep=""), "plots"),
                         height = 8,
                         width = 6,
                         plot = plotlist_with_stats[[x]]))

## Phenotyping Frequency ggstatplots

data_phen_all_selected <- data_phen_all %>%
  filter(Experiment == 3 & Donor != "SAC253053" & Group != "Vein PBMC Pre") %>%
  filter(Group == "Tasso WB" | Group == "Tasso WB Shipped") %>%
  filter(Donor %in% filtered_tasso_id)

table(data_phen_all_selected$Donor, data_phen_all_selected$Group)

plot_cols_names <- c(plot_phen_cols_names, "Count")

for (i in unique(data_phen_all_selected$Population)){
  print(i)
  
  data.plot <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  current_plot_cols_names <- plot_cols_names[plot_cols_names %notin% plot_cols_names[sapply(data.plot[plot_cols_names], sum_rm_na) == 0]] #removes columns where the selected samples have no values for a parameter
  
    current_plot_cols_names <- plot_cols_names[plot_cols_names %notin% plot_cols_names[sapply(data.plot[plot_cols_names], sd) == 0]] #removes columns where there is no differences, messes up ggstatplot otherwise
  
  # plotlist <- purrr::pmap(
  #   .l = list(
  #     data = list(data.plot),
  #     x = "Group",
  #     y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
  #     title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, "      Population: ", i, sep="")),
  #     type = "nonparametric",
  #     plot.type = "box",
  #     pairwise.display = "none",
  #     package = "ggsci",
  #     palette = "default_igv",
  #     silent = TRUE,
  #     point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
  #     centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
  #     violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
  #     ggplot.component = list(list(
  #     geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
  #     ylim(0,NA)
  #     )
  #   )),
  #   .f = ggwithinstats
  # )
  
  plotlist_with_stats <- purrr::pmap(
    .l = list(
      data = list(data.plot),
      x = "Group",
      y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
      title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, "      Population: ", i, sep="")),
      type = "nonparametric",
      plot.type = "box",
      pairwise.display = "all",
      package = "ggsci",
      palette = "default_igv",
      silent = TRUE,
      point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
      centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
      violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
      ggplot.component = list(list(
      geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
      ylim(0,100)
      )
    )),
    .f = ggwithinstats
  )
  
  # names(plotlist) <- current_plot_cols_names
  names(plotlist_with_stats) <- current_plot_cols_names
  
  stat_list <- lapply(plotlist_with_stats, extract_stats)
  
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep="")))
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""),"stat_tables"))
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""),"plots"))
  
  for (x in names(stat_list)){
  # print(x)
  temp <- stat_list[[x]]$subtitle_data
  write_csv(temp,
            file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis,
                             paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""),
                             "stat_tables",
                             paste(i, " ", x, ".csv", sep = ""))
  )
}
  
  # lapply(names(plotlist),
  #        function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(i, " ", x, ".pdf", 
  #                                           sep=""),
  #                          path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"),
  #                          height = 8,
  #                          width = 6, 
  #                          plot = plotlist[[x]]))
  
  lapply(names(plotlist_with_stats),
         function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(i, " ", x, "_with_stats.pdf",
                                            sep=""),
                           path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"),
                           height = 8,
                           width = 6,
                           plot = plotlist_with_stats[[x]]))
}

write.csv(data_frequency_all_selected,
          file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, "data_frequency_all_selected.csv"))

write.csv(data_phen_all_selected,
          file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, "data_phen_all_selected.csv"))

```

```{r comparisons_4_2_F_Vein_WB_v_Tasso_WB}

## Comparison 4_2_F: Individual testing using ggstatplots with cell filter applied to phenotyping outputs

## Phenotyping Frequency ggstatplots

dir.plots_analysis <- file.path(dir.plots,"Comparison_4_2_F_Vein_WB_v_Tasso_WB")
dir.create(dir.plots_analysis)

data_phen_all_selected <- data_phen_all %>%
  filter(Experiment == 3 & Donor != "SAC253053" & Group != "Vein PBMC Pre") %>%
  filter(Group == "Vein WB" | Group == "Tasso WB") %>%
  filter(Donor %in% filtered_tasso_id)

### Filter out low cell count sets

sets_to_remove <- unique(data_phen_all_selected[data_phen_all_selected$Filter == TRUE,]$Donor_Population_Date)

data_phen_all_selected <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
  filter(Donor_Population_Date %notin% sets_to_remove)

### Filter out low cell count sets

table(data_phen_all_selected$Donor, data_phen_all_selected$Group)
table(data_phen_all_selected$Population, data_phen_all_selected$Group)

plot_cols_names <- c(plot_phen_cols_names, "Count")

for (i in unique(data_phen_all_selected$Population)){
  print(i)
  
  data.plot <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  current_plot_cols_names <- plot_cols_names[plot_cols_names %notin% plot_cols_names[sapply(data.plot[plot_cols_names], sum_rm_na) == 0]] #removes columns where the selected samples have no values for a parameter
  
    current_plot_cols_names <- plot_cols_names[plot_cols_names %notin% plot_cols_names[sapply(data.plot[plot_cols_names], sd) == 0]] #removes columns where there is no differences, messes up ggstatplot otherwise
  
  # plotlist <- purrr::pmap(
  #   .l = list(
  #     data = list(data.plot),
  #     x = "Group",
  #     y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
  #     title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, "      Population: ", i,  " Filtered",sep="")),
  #     type = "nonparametric",
  #     plot.type = "box",
  #     pairwise.display = "none",
  #     package = "ggsci",
  #     palette = "default_igv",
  #     silent = TRUE,
  #     point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
  #     centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
  #     violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
  #     ggplot.component = list(list(
  #     geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
  #     ylim(0,NA)
  #     )
  #   )),
  #   .f = ggwithinstats
  # )
  
  plotlist_with_stats <- purrr::pmap(
    .l = list(
      data = list(data.plot),
      x = "Group",
      y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
      title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, "      Population: ", i,  " Filtered", sep="")),
      type = "nonparametric",
      plot.type = "box",
      pairwise.display = "all",
      package = "ggsci",
      palette = "default_igv",
      silent = TRUE,
      point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
      centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
      violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
      ggplot.component = list(list(
      geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
      ylim(0,NA)
      )
    )),
    .f = ggwithinstats
  )
  
  # names(plotlist) <- current_plot_cols_names
  names(plotlist_with_stats) <- current_plot_cols_names
  
  stat_list <- lapply(plotlist_with_stats, extract_stats)
  
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep="")))
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "stat_tables"))
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"))
  
for (x in names(stat_list)){
  # print(x)
  temp <- stat_list[[x]]$subtitle_data
  write_csv(temp,
            file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis,
                             paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""),
                             "stat_tables",
                             paste(i, " ", x, ".csv", sep = ""))
  )
}
  
  # lapply(names(plotlist),
  #        function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(i, " ", x, "Filtered.pdf", 
  #                                           sep=""),
  #                          path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"),
  #                          height = 8,
  #                          width = 12, 
  #                          plot = plotlist[[x]]))
  
  lapply(names(plotlist_with_stats),
         function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(i, " ", x, "_with_stats_Filtered.pdf",
                                            sep=""),
                           path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"),
                           height = 8,
                           width = 12,
                           plot = plotlist_with_stats[[x]]))
}

write.csv(data_phen_all_selected,
          file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, "data_phen_all_selected.csv"))

```

```{r comparisons_4_2_F_Vein_PBMC_v_Vein_WB}

## Comparison 4_2_F: Individual testing using ggstatplots with cell filter applied to phenotyping outputs

## Phenotyping Frequency ggstatplots

dir.plots_analysis <- file.path(dir.plots,"Comparison_4_2_F_Vein_PBMC_v_Vein_WB")
dir.create(dir.plots_analysis)

data_phen_all_selected <- data_phen_all %>%
  filter(Experiment == 3 & Donor != "SAC253053" & Group != "Vein PBMC Pre") %>%
  filter(Group == "Vein PBMC" | Group == "Vein WB") %>%
  filter(Donor %in% filtered_tasso_id)

### Filter out low cell count sets

sets_to_remove <- unique(data_phen_all_selected[data_phen_all_selected$Filter == TRUE,]$Donor_Population_Date)

data_phen_all_selected <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
  filter(Donor_Population_Date %notin% sets_to_remove)

### Filter out low cell count sets

table(data_phen_all_selected$Donor, data_phen_all_selected$Group)
table(data_phen_all_selected$Population, data_phen_all_selected$Group)

plot_cols_names <- c(plot_phen_cols_names, "Count")

for (i in unique(data_phen_all_selected$Population)){
  print(i)
  
  data.plot <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  current_plot_cols_names <- plot_cols_names[plot_cols_names %notin% plot_cols_names[sapply(data.plot[plot_cols_names], sum_rm_na) == 0]] #removes columns where the selected samples have no values for a parameter
  
    current_plot_cols_names <- plot_cols_names[plot_cols_names %notin% plot_cols_names[sapply(data.plot[plot_cols_names], sd) == 0]] #removes columns where there is no differences, messes up ggstatplot otherwise

  #     x = "Group",
  #     y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
  #     title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, "      Population: ", i,  " Filtered",sep="")),
  #     type = "nonparametric",

  #     centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),

  #     ggplot.component = list(list(
  #     geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
  #     ylim(0,NA)
  #     )
  #   )),
  #   .f = ggwithinstats
  # )
  
  plotlist_with_stats <- purrr::pmap(
    .l = list(
      data = list(data.plot),
      x = "Group",
      y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
      title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, "      Population: ", i,  " Filtered", sep="")),
      type = "nonparametric",
      plot.type = "box",
      pairwise.display = "all",
      package = "ggsci",
      palette = "default_igv",
      silent = TRUE,
      point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
      centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
      violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
      ggplot.component = list(list(
      geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
      ylim(0,NA)
      )
    )),
    .f = ggwithinstats
  )
  
  # names(plotlist) <- current_plot_cols_names
  names(plotlist_with_stats) <- current_plot_cols_names
  
  stat_list <- lapply(plotlist_with_stats, extract_stats)
  
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep="")))
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "stat_tables"))
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"))
  
for (x in names(stat_list)){
  # print(x)
  temp <- stat_list[[x]]$subtitle_data
  write_csv(temp,
            file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis,
                             paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""),
                             "stat_tables",
                             paste(i, " ", x, ".csv", sep = ""))
  )
}
  
  # lapply(names(plotlist),
  #        function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(i, " ", x, "Filtered.pdf", 
  #                                           sep=""),
  #                          path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"),
  #                          height = 8,
  #                          width = 12, 
  #                          plot = plotlist[[x]]))
  
  lapply(names(plotlist_with_stats),
         function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(i, " ", x, "_with_stats_Filtered.pdf",
                                            sep=""),
                           path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"),
                           height = 8,
                           width = 12,
                           plot = plotlist_with_stats[[x]]))
}

          file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, "data_phen_all_selected.csv"))
```

```{r comparisons_4_2_F_Tasso_WB_v_Tasso_WB_Shipped}

## Comparison 4_2_F: Individual testing using ggstatplots with cell filter applied to phenotyping outputs

## Phenotyping Frequency ggstatplots

dir.plots_analysis <- file.path(dir.plots,"Comparison_4_2_F_Tasso_WB_v_Tasso_WB_Shipped")
dir.create(dir.plots_analysis)

data_phen_all_selected <- data_phen_all %>%
  filter(Experiment == 3 & Donor != "SAC253053" & Group != "Vein PBMC Pre") %>%
  filter(Group == "Tasso WB" | Group == "Tasso WB Shipped") %>%
  filter(Donor %in% filtered_tasso_id)

### Filter out low cell count sets

sets_to_remove <- unique(data_phen_all_selected[data_phen_all_selected$Filter == TRUE,]$Donor_Population_Date)

data_phen_all_selected <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
  filter(Donor_Population_Date %notin% sets_to_remove)

### Filter out low cell count sets

table(data_phen_all_selected$Donor, data_phen_all_selected$Group)
table(data_phen_all_selected$Population, data_phen_all_selected$Group)

plot_cols_names <- c(plot_phen_cols_names, "Count")

for (i in unique(data_phen_all_selected$Population)){
  print(i)
  
  data.plot <- data_phen_all_selected %>%
    filter(Population == i)
  
  current_plot_cols_names <- plot_cols_names[plot_cols_names %notin% plot_cols_names[sapply(data.plot[plot_cols_names], sum_rm_na) == 0]] #removes columns where the selected samples have no values for a parameter
  
    current_plot_cols_names <- plot_cols_names[plot_cols_names %notin% plot_cols_names[sapply(data.plot[plot_cols_names], sd) == 0]] #removes columns where there is no differences, messes up ggstatplot otherwise
  
  # plotlist <- purrr::pmap(
  #   .l = list(
  #     data = list(data.plot),
  #     x = "Group",
  #     y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
  #     title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, "      Population: ", i,  " Filtered",sep="")),
  #     type = "nonparametric",
  #     plot.type = "box",
  #     pairwise.display = "none",
  #     package = "ggsci",
  #     palette = "default_igv",
  #     silent = TRUE,
  #     point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
  #     centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
  #     violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
  #     ggplot.component = list(list(
  #     geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
  #     ylim(0,NA)
  #     )
  #   )),
  #   .f = ggwithinstats
  # )
  
  plotlist_with_stats <- purrr::pmap(
    .l = list(
      data = list(data.plot),
      x = "Group",
      y = as.list(current_plot_cols_names),
      title = as.list(paste("Parameter: ", current_plot_cols_names, "      Population: ", i,  " Filtered", sep="")),
      type = "nonparametric",
      plot.type = "box",
      pairwise.display = "all",
      package = "ggsci",
      palette = "default_igv",
      silent = TRUE,
      point.args = list(list(alpha = 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
      centrality.point.args = list(list(alpha = 0)),
      violin.args = list(list(width = 0, linewidth = 0)),
      ggplot.component = list(list(
      geom_point(aes(color = data.plot$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0),
      ylim(0,NA)
      )
    )),
    .f = ggwithinstats
  )
  
  # names(plotlist) <- current_plot_cols_names
  names(plotlist_with_stats) <- current_plot_cols_names
  
  stat_list <- lapply(plotlist_with_stats, extract_stats)
  
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep="")))
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "stat_tables"))
  dir.create(file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"))
  
for (x in names(stat_list)){
  # print(x)
  temp <- stat_list[[x]]$subtitle_data
  write_csv(temp,
            file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis,
                             paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""),
                             "stat_tables",
                             paste(i, " ", x, ".csv", sep = ""))
  )
}
  
  # lapply(names(plotlist),
  #        function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(i, " ", x, "Filtered.pdf", 
  #                                           sep=""),
  #                          path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"),
  #                          height = 8,
  #                          width = 12, 
  #                          plot = plotlist[[x]]))
  
  lapply(names(plotlist_with_stats),
         function(x)ggsave(filename = paste(i, " ", x, "_with_stats_Filtered.pdf",
                                            sep=""),
                           path = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, paste("plots_ggwithinstats_", i, sep=""), "plots"),
                           height = 8,
                           width = 12,
                           plot = plotlist_with_stats[[x]]))
}

write.csv(data_phen_all_selected,
          file = file.path(dir.plots_analysis, "data_phen_all_selected.csv"))

```

# General Plot

```{r general_plots}

data_frequency_all_selected <- data_frequency_all %>%
  filter(Group == "Tasso WB Shipped" & Experiment == "3")

ggscatterstats_meta_time_transit <- ggscatterstats(
  data = data_frequency_all_selected,
  type = "parametric",
  bf.message = FALSE,
  # results.subtitle = FALSE,
  marginal = FALSE,
  binwidth = 1,
  x = `Transit Days`, 
  y = Live_Percent_of_Single_Cells,
  title = "Blank",
  point.args = list(alpha = 0, stroke = 0),
  smooth.line.args = list(linewidth = 0.2, color = "blue", method = "lm", formula = y ~ x),
  ggplot.component = list(
        geom_point(aes(color = data_frequency_all_selected$Donor), size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0, show.legend = FALSE),
        donor_colors,
        ylim(0,105),
        theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 6),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 6),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 6),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6)
      ),
      ylab("% of Singlets"))
      )

ggscatterstats_meta_time_transit

ggsave(filename = paste("Cohort of 16 Donors - Time of Shipped Tasso Device in Transit by Viability 1.pdf"),
         plot = ggscatterstats_meta_time_transit,
         path = dir.plots,
         width = 2,
         height = 1.4)


ggscatterstats_meta_time_transit <- ggscatterstats(
  data = data_frequency_all_selected, 
  type = "parametric",
  binwidth = 1,
  x = `Transit Days`, 
  y = Live_Percent_of_Single_Cells,
  title = "Cohort of 16 Donors - Time of Shipped Tasso Device in Transit by Viability",
  point.args = list(alpha = 0, stroke = 0),
  ggplot.component = list(
        geom_point(aes(color = data_frequency_all_selected$Donor), size = 5, alpha = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0, show.legend = FALSE),
        ylim(0,105)
      )
)

ggscatterstats_meta_time_transit

         plot = ggscatterstats_meta_time_transit,

         height = 8)

  ggplot.component = list(
        geom_point(aes(color = data_frequency_all_selected$Donor), size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6, na.rm = TRUE,  stroke = 0, show.legend = FALSE),
        donor_colors,
        ylim(0,105),
        theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 6),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 6),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 6),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6)
      ),
      ylab("% of Singlets"))
      )

ggscatterstats_meta_mean_temp_transit

ggsave(filename = paste("Cohort of 16 Donors - Mean Temperature of Shipped Tasso Device in Transit by Viability 1.pdf"),
         plot = ggscatterstats_meta_mean_temp_transit,
         path = dir.plots,
         width = 12,
         height = 8)


ggscatterstats_meta_mean_temp_transit <- ggscatterstats(
  data = data_frequency_all_selected,
```
